      Survey Submitted

      📋
      Instructions & Guide
      Everything you need to complete your market survey
      👋
      Welcome
      What is this portal and how does it work?

      The Berger Communities Market Survey Portal is where your team collects competitive pricing data each month. It replaces spreadsheets and emails with one simple, consistent process across the portfolio.

      📊 Monthly Survey

      This is what you fill out every month. It captures current pricing, occupancy, and concessions for your property and each of your properties.

      🗂️ Annual Comp Profile

      Complete for your subject property and each competitor in your market. This sets up your database — amenities, fees, utilities, and property details. The subject profile enables side-by-side comparison on the dashboard.

      First time using the portal? Start with the Annual Profile. You need to complete two types of profiles: one for your subject property (your Berger property) and one for each competitor in your market. Use the Subject / Competitor toggle at the top of the Annual Profile form. Once all profiles are saved, your Monthly Survey checklist will be ready to go. Return any time to update a profile when you notice changes — a new amenity, a fee increase, a renovation. Think of it as a living record, not a once-a-year task.
      📊
      How to Complete Your Monthly Survey
      Follow these steps each month
      1
      Select your survey details
      Choose the survey month, type your name, and select your property from the dropdown. Your properties will appear as a checklist automatically.
      2
      Select your properties
      Check the properties you are surveying this month. You can also type in a new comp name if needed. Then click Apply Selection to build your survey.
      3
      Fill in your subject property first
      Enter your property's current occupancy and any active concessions. Then add a row for each unit type — include the unit label, beds, baths, Classic or Renovation, and the best price (lowest price offered). Use the ★ star to mark your primary unit type. Note the lease term in your notes.
      4
      Complete each comp block
      Work through each property one at a time. Enter their occupancy, concessions, and unit type pricing. Select where you got the data from (Phone, CoStar, Website, or Other). If a property won't share pricing, click Yes — could not collect and note the reason.
      5
      Save your progress if needed
      Click 💾 Save Draft any time to save your work to this device. If you close the tab and come back later, click 📂 Load Draft to pick up right where you left off. Note: drafts are saved to your browser only and will not transfer to another device or browser.
      6
      Mark a primary unit type for each floorplan
      Click the ★ star to mark one primary unit type per bedroom count (e.g., one primary for Studio, one for 1 Bedroom, one for 2 Bedroom). The primary row is used on the dashboard for direct rent comparison.
      7
      Use Load Last Month to save time
      Click 🔄 Load Last Month to pull in your unit type rows from your previous submission. You only need to update the pricing — no need to re-enter unit types every month.
      8
      Submit your survey
      When you are done, click Submit Survey at the bottom. You will see a confirmation screen with a timestamp confirming your submission was recorded.
      🗂️
      How to Complete an Annual Comp Profile
      Update at least annually — or any time something changes
      ⚠️ Important: You Must Complete Profiles for BOTH Your Subject Property AND Your Competitors
      The Annual Profile form has a Subject / Competitor toggle at the top. You need to fill out one profile with "Subject" selected (for your Berger property) and then separate profiles with "Competitor" selected for each comp in your market. The subject profile captures your property's amenities, fees, and utilities so the dashboard can compare you side-by-side against competitors.
      1
      Choose Subject or Competitor
      At the top of the form, select whether you are profiling your subject property (your Berger property) or a competitor. When Subject is selected, the property name and address auto-fill. Fill out all the same fields — amenities, fees, utilities — so the dashboard can compare apples to apples.
      2
      Select your property
      Choose your Berger property from the dropdown. Your property address and submarket will fill in automatically.
      3
      Enter the property details
      Fill in the property's name, address, total units, year built, quality tier, product type, and management company. Enter the distance from your property in miles. The more complete this is, the more useful your monthly surveys will be.
      4
      Complete the amenities section
      Check every amenity that the property offers. For fees, deposits, and utilities, enter the dollar amounts or select how residents are billed. Be as thorough as possible. If a property adds an amenity, changes their fees, or renovates units, come back and update this section right away — do not wait for the annual cycle.
      5
      Add photos
      Drag and drop photos of the property — exterior, amenities, and unit interiors if you have them. This helps leadership understand the competitive context.
      6
      Write your observations
      Add notes on the property's strengths, weaknesses, and anything else worth knowing. This is your chance to share what you see on the ground — be specific and honest.
      7
      Submit the profile
      Click Submit to save the profile. This property will now appear on your monthly survey checklist automatically. Come back and update this profile any time you notice changes at the property — a new amenity, a fee change, a renovation — not just at the annual review.
      💡
      Tips for Getting Good Data
      💻 Check the website first
      The property website and ILS listings are your primary sources. Check these first for current pricing and availability before using any other source.
      ⭐ Use the star to mark your primary unit
      The star marks the unit type that best competes head-to-head with your subject property. This is used for direct comparisons in reporting.
      🏷️ Keep unit labels consistent
      Use the same unit type labels every month — for example, always "1BR Classic" rather than switching between "1 bed" or "1BD." Consistent labels make month-over-month comparison possible.
      🚫 Mark Unable to Collect — don't leave it blank
      If a property refuses to share pricing, use the Unable to Collect toggle instead of leaving the block empty. Blank data looks the same as missing data in reports.
      🔄 Use Load Last Month
      Don't re-enter your unit types every month. Click Load Last Month to bring in your previous rows and just update the numbers. It saves a lot of time.
      ❓
      Common Questions
      My property is not showing up on the checklist
      You need to complete an Annual Comp Profile for that property first. Once you submit it, the property will appear on your monthly checklist automatically.
      I started a survey but had to stop — will I lose my work?
      Click Save Draft before you close the tab. When you come back, click Load Draft and everything will be restored. Just make sure you are on the same device and browser.
      A property has closed down or I no longer track them
      Open their Annual Comp Profile, check the No Longer Tracking box at the top, and submit. They will be removed from your monthly checklist going forward.
      My draft is not loading
      Drafts are saved to your browser only. If you are using a private or incognito window, or have recently cleared your browser history, the draft may not be available.
      I submitted something by mistake or with incorrect data
      Contact Hadley McKnight to correct the record. Include your property name, the survey month, and what needs to be fixed.
      For any questions please reach out to Hadley McKnight
